---
title: County Assessment Maps
output:
  html_document:
      css: style.css
date: "Report generated on `r Sys.Date()`"
params:
  data: NA
---

<style type="text/css">
/* Force the R Markdown container to use 95% of the screen width */
.main-container {
  max-width: 95% !important;
  margin: auto;
}
</style>

```{r setup, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Load only the packages required for visualization and presentation
pacman::p_load("leaflet", "dplyr", "DT", "sf", "htmltools")

if(FALSE){
  # Unpack the pre-processed data bundle
meta   <- params$data$meta
layers <- params$data$map_layers
tables <- params$data$tables
}else{
  #testing 
meta   <- data$meta
layers <- data$map_layers
tables <- data$tables
}






```

<img src="logobanner.png" alt="Supporting Organizations" style="width: 100%">
<br>

<h1 style="text-align: center;"> `r meta$speciesName` </h1>

<strong style="text-align: center;">This project is a collaboration among the <a href="https://www.usbg.gov/">United States Botanic Garden</a>, <a href="https://sdbg.org/">San Diego Botanic Garden</a>, <a href="https://www.ars.usda.gov/">USDA ARS</a>, <a href="https://gis.colostate.edu/">Geospatial Centroid</a> at Colorado State University, and <a href="https://www.natureserve.org/">NatureServe</a>. We are all grateful for your time and consideration in this evaluation effort. </strong>

<a href="https://geospatialcentroid.github.io/vitis/home" target="_blank">Vitis Home Page</a>
<br>
<a href="https://geospatialcentroid.github.io/vitis/countyReports" target="_blank">All Vitis County Maps</a>
<br>

*If you are evaluating these maps, please be sure to complete the initial <a href="https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeyoQC-Ta2-KP-rGiGVqcZ3jq5AnpN6FdFptGcoCuOAteKYuw/viewform?usp=sf_link" target="_blank">registration form</a>.*
<br>
<a href="https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1fROduCBuYkKUv9bd1_N9SJ8ijyaA0C5BqG9nKZHFigI/edit?usp=sharing" target="_blank">Listing of all occurrence records for all species</a>
<br> 
<a href="https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1m6toqYmv49nz9A029p4DrkuQL7sis04wHZH1RzgsXdg/edit?usp=sharing" target="_blank">Listing of all USDA Plants and Bonap records</a>

## Supporting information on method and taxonomy {.tabset}

### Taxonomic concept

<br>
**Taxon** : `r meta$speciesName`
<br>
**Accepted Synonym(s)** : `r meta$synonyms$acceptedSynonym`
<br> 
**Excluded from concept** : `r meta$synonyms$Names_to_exclude_from_this_Species_Complex`
<br>
**NatureServe Concept Reference**: `r meta$synonyms$NatureServe_Concept_reference`
<br>
**Relationship to Flora of North America**: `r meta$synonyms$Relationship_to_FNA`
<br>
**Taxonomic Notes**: `r meta$synonyms$Taxonomic_notes`
<br> 
**NatureServe Classification Status**: `r meta$synonyms$Classification_Status_NatureServe`
<br>
**NatureServe Distribution**: `r meta$nsRef$Distribution`
<br>
**NatureServe Explorer**: `r meta$nsRef$link`
<br>
**This species is included in FNA : `r meta$fnaIncluded`**
<br> 

### Details on the map elements 

#### County Level Features 

All county level features report information that as aggregated to the county level. This include text reference to the county (state/county) and spatial point reference that occur within the county. 

- <strong> Counties with a record: </strong> This presence absence map shows a county as green if it has at least one record from any of the evaluated sources.
- <strong> Sum of records within County: </strong> This layer depicts the total number of unique records of a species across all sources within a county.
- <strong> Counties listed in USDA Plants and/or Bonap Only (No occurrence points) </strong> These counties only contain a reference from USDA Plants and/or BONAP. There are no identified iNaturalist, herbarium, or germplasm occurrences from these locations.
- <strong> Counties with one or more Herbarium or Germplasm Occurrence: </strong> These counties have at least one herbarium and/or germplasm occurrence. There may also be additional records within this county(iNaturalist, USDA Plants, BONAP, NatureServe).
- <strong> Counties with iNaturalist Observation only:  </strong> These counties only have species observations from iNaturalist.

#### Occurrence Features

-  <strong>Germplasm Occurrences: </strong> Occurrence data with living specimens stored in a Genebank or Botanical Garden.
-  <strong>Herbarium Occurrences with web url: </strong> Occurrence data of herbarium specimens, which includes an url for the source herbarium data.  
-  <strong>Herbarium Occurrences without web url:</strong> Occurrence data of herbarium specimens.
-  <strong>iNaturalist observation data:</strong> Research grade observations from iNaturalist. 

### General Methods 

#### Gathering Data

Data was gathered at the Genus level from the following 

**public repositories**
- [Seinet](https://swbiodiversity.org/seinet/) 
- [Genesys](https://www.genesys-pgr.org/)
- [Wiews](https://www.fao.org/wiews/en/)
- [GRIN](https://www.ars-grin.gov/) 
- [USDA Plants](https://plants.usda.gov/home) 
- [Crop Wild Relative 2020 publication](https://dataverse.harvard.edu/dataset.xhtml?persistentId=doi:10.7910/DVN/BV4I06) 

**Sourced through communication with data owners** - [2023 Botanical Garden Survey](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1fROduCBuYkKUv9bd1_N9SJ8ijyaA0C5BqG9nKZHFigI/edit?usp=sharing) -- available in the full source data 
- [BONAP](http://www.bonap.org/)
- [IUCN](https://www.iucnredlist.org/)

#### Data Standardization and Taxonomy 
**data standardization** : All data sets were altered to matched a standard data structure. The specific translation of data sets can be seen [here](https://github.com/dcarver1/cwr_wildgrapes/tree/main/preprocessing). 

**Taxonomy**: The full synonyms cross walk can be viewed [here](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1ZzKUr2GI8wZe42G-nnMZ3iyhUnnqn3OUCMK_0sFKniQ/edit?usp=drive_link). 

#### Assigning the State and County to each occurrence 
When latitude and longitude data was used to assign the state/country when possible. If no latitude and longitude data was provided, the state and county names were pulled from the attribute data associated with the record. If a state or county name could not be assigned by either method the record was excluded from this analysis. 

#### Flora of North America 
If state level distribution information was provided for a given specific species, that information was used to filter the states in which counties are evaluated. 

## Map of County and Occurrence Records

```{r produceMAP, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

if(nrow(layers$points) == 0){
  print("There is no spatial data for this species and no map can be produced.")
} else {

  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # 1. GENERATE DYNAMIC POPUPS AND URLS
  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  
  # Point layer popups
  layers$points <- layers$points |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      link = paste0("[https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScCTcg6ITFMXs9QpQEAvN92GI06wtSTiGSTcUBrOK6E2kChjw/viewform?usp=pp_url&entry.1016353547=](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScCTcg6ITFMXs9QpQEAvN92GI06wtSTiGSTcUBrOK6E2kChjw/viewform?usp=pp_url&entry.1016353547=)",
                    taxon, "&entry.764274277=", countyFIPS, "&entry.763749150=", state,
                    "&entry.1585476570=", county, "&entry.1304312867=", recordID),
      url_formatted = dplyr::case_when(
        grepl("http", x = sourceUniqueID) ~ paste0("<a target='_blank' href=\"", sourceUniqueID, "\">view</a>"),
        grepl("http", x = collectionSource) ~ paste0("<a target='_blank' href=\"", collectionSource, "\">view</a>"),
        TRUE ~ "NA"
      ),
      popup = paste0(
        "<b>Taxon: </b>", taxon,
        "<br/> <b>Data Source: </b>", databaseSource,
        "<br/> <b> Record ID: </b>", sourceUniqueID,
        "<br/> <b> Type: </b>", type,
        "<br/> <b> Year: </b>", yearRecorded,
        "<br/> <b> Recorder Name: </b>", observerName,
        "<br/> <b> Location Description: </b>", localityInformation,
        "<br/> <b> External Reference: </b>", url_formatted,
        "<br/> <b> Report Issue: </b><a target='_blank' href=\"", link, "\">click here</a>"
      )
    )

  # Function to generate county popups so we can apply it to all county sub-layers
  generate_county_popup <- function(df) {
    if (nrow(df) == 0) return(df)
    df |>
      dplyr::mutate(
        # FIX: Changed FIPS to fips2 to match your original data structure
        link = paste0("https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScCTcg6ITFMXs9QpQEAvN92GI06wtSTiGSTcUBrOK6E2kChjw/viewform?usp=pp_url&entry.1016353547=",
                      meta$speciesName, "&entry.764274277=", GEOID,
                      "&entry.763749150=", STUSPS, "&entry.1585476570=", NAME, "&entry.1304312867=", "NA"),
        USDAPlantsPop = dplyr::case_when(`USDA Plants` == 1 ~ "Yes", TRUE ~ "No"),
        BONAPPop = dplyr::case_when(`BONAP` == 1 ~ "Yes", TRUE ~ "No"),
        NatureServePop = dplyr::case_when(`Nature Serve` == 1 ~ "Yes", TRUE ~ "No"),
        popup = paste0(
          "<b>County Name: </b>", NAME,
          "<br/> <b>State: </b>", STUSPS,
          "<br/> <b> Listed in USDA Plants: </b>", USDAPlantsPop,
          "<br/> <b> Listed in BONAP: </b>", BONAPPop,
          "<br/> <b> Listed in Nature Serve: </b>", NatureServePop,
          "<br/> <b> Number of observations records: </b>", O,
          "<br/> <b> Number of herbarium records: </b>", H,
          "<br/> <b> Number of germplasm records: </b>", G,
          "<br/> <b> Number of records before 1970: </b>", pre1970,
          "<br/> <b> All Records: </b>", anyRecord,
          "<br/> <b> Report Issue: </b><a target='_blank' href=\"", link, "\">click here</a>"
        )
      )
  }
  # Apply popup generation to all county layers
  layers$countyClass <- generate_county_popup(layers$countyClass)
  layers$bpData      <- generate_county_popup(layers$bpData)
  layers$hgData      <- generate_county_popup(layers$hgData)
  layers$oData       <- generate_county_popup(layers$oData)


  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # 2. BUILD THE MAP
  # ---------------------------------------------------------

  # Base Map Setup
  rr <- tags$div(HTML(paste0("<h3>", meta$speciesName , "</h3>")))  

  m1 <- leaflet(width = "100%", height = 800, options = leafletOptions(minZoom = 3)) |>
    addProviderTiles(providers$Esri.WorldGrayCanvas) |>
    addMapPane("anyRecord", zIndex = 414) |>
    addMapPane("sumOfRecords", zIndex = 415) |>
    addMapPane("bpData", zIndex = 418) |>
    addMapPane("hgData", zIndex = 419) |>
    addMapPane("points", zIndex = 420) |>
    addControl(rr, position = "bottomleft")

  # Add Spatial Features
  m2 <- m1 |>
    addPolygons(data = layers$countyClass,
                color = "white", fillColor = ~singleColor, popup = ~popup,
                weight = 0.8, fillOpacity = 0.6, opacity = 0.8,
                group = "Counties with a record", options = pathOptions(pane = "anyRecord")) |>
    addPolygons(data = layers$countyClass,
                color = "white", fillColor = ~allColor, popup = ~popup,
                weight = 0.8, fillOpacity = 1, opacity = 0.8,
                group = "Sum of records within County", options = pathOptions(pane = "sumOfRecords")) |>
    addPolygons(data = layers$bpData,
                color = "white", fillColor = "#fec44f", popup = ~popup,
                weight = 0.8, fillOpacity = 1, opacity = 0.8,
                group = "Counties listed in USDA Plants and/or Bonap Only (No occurrence points)", options = pathOptions(pane = "bpData")) |>
    addPolygons(data = layers$hgData,
                color = "white", fillColor = "#fa9fb5", popup = ~popup,
                weight = 0.8, fillOpacity = 1, opacity = 0.8,
                group = "Counties with one or more Herbarium or Germplasm Occurrence", options = pathOptions(pane = "hgData")) |>
    addPolygons(data = layers$oData,
                color = "white", fillColor = "#f03b20", popup = ~popup,
                weight = 0.8, fillOpacity = 1, opacity = 0.8,
                group = "Counties with iNaturalist Observation only", options = pathOptions(pane = "hgData")) |>
    addCircleMarkers(data = layers$points |> filter(type == "O"),
                     color = ~color, opacity = 1, radius = 2, stroke = 1, popup = ~popup,
                     group = "iNaturalist observation data", options = pathOptions(pane = "points")) |>
    addCircleMarkers(data = layers$points |> filter(type == "H" & is.na(url)),
                     color = "#fa9fb5", opacity = 1, radius = 2, stroke = 1, popup = ~popup,
                     group = "Herbarium Occurrences without web url", options = pathOptions(pane = "points")) |>
    addCircleMarkers(data = layers$points |> filter(type == "H" & !is.na(url)),
                     color = "#c51b8a", opacity = 1, radius = 2, stroke = 1, popup = ~popup,
                     group = "Herbarium Occurrences with web url", options = pathOptions(pane = "points")) |>
    addCircleMarkers(data = layers$points |> filter(type == "G"),
                     color = ~color, opacity = 1, radius = 2, stroke = 1, popup = ~popup,
                     group = "Germplasm Occurrences", options = pathOptions(pane = "points"))

  # Add Legends and Layer Controls
  brbgAll <- c("#a6611a", "#dfc27d", '#c7eae5', '#80cdc1', '#35978f', '#01665e')
  allVals <- c("1", "2-3", "3-5", "5-8", "8-11", ">12")

  m3 <- m2 |>
    addLegend(color = "#a8ddb5", group = "Counties with a record", labels = "Counties with a record") |>
    addLegend(color = brbgAll, labels = allVals, group = "Sum of records within County", title = "Sum of records within County") |>
    addLegend(color = "#fec44f", group = "Counties listed in USDA Plants and/or Bonap Only (No occurrence points)", labels = "Counties listed in USDA Plants and/or Bonap Only (No occurrence points)") |>
    addLegend(color = "#fa9fb5", group = "Counties with one or more Herbarium or Germplasm Occurrence", label = "Counties with one or more Herbarium or Germplasm Occurrence") |>
    addLegend(color = "#f03b20", group = "Counties with iNaturalist Observation only", labels = "Counties with iNaturalist Observation only") |>
    addLegend(color = "#66c2a5", group = "Germplasm Occurrences", labels = "Germplasm Occurrences") |>
    addLegend(color = "#c51b8a", group = "Herbarium Occurrences with web url", labels = "Herbarium Occurrences with web url") |>
    addLegend(color = "#fa9fb5", group = "Herbarium Occurrences without web url", labels = "Herbarium Occurrences without web url") |>
    addLegend(color = "#fc8d62", group = "iNaturalist observation data", labels = "iNaturalist observation data") |>
    addLayersControl(
      overlayGroups = c("Counties with a record", "Sum of records within County", 
                        "Counties listed in USDA Plants and/or Bonap Only (No occurrence points)",
                        "Counties with one or more Herbarium or Germplasm Occurrence",
                        "Counties with iNaturalist Observation only", "counties changed during review",
                        "Germplasm Occurrences", "Herbarium Occurrences with web url",
                        "Herbarium Occurrences without web url", "iNaturalist observation data",
                        "Locations changed during review"),
      options = layersControlOptions(collapsed = FALSE)
    ) |>
    hideGroup(c("Sum of records within County", "Counties listed in USDA Plants and/or Bonap Only (No occurrence points)",
                "Counties with one or more Herbarium or Germplasm Occurrence", "Counties with iNaturalist Observation only", 
                "Herbarium Occurrences with web url", "Herbarium Occurrences without web url",
                "iNaturalist observation data", "Germplasm Occurrences"))

  # Add Reviewed Data if it exists
  if(nrow(layers$rCounty) > 0){
    m3 <- m3 |>
      addPolygons(data = layers$rCounty, color = "black", fillColor = "#ebfc03", popup = ~popup,
                  weight = 0.8, fillOpacity = 1, opacity = 0.8,
                  group = "counties changed during review", options = pathOptions(pane = "hgData"))
  }

  if(nrow(layers$rPoints) > 0){
    m3 <- m3 |>
      addCircleMarkers(data = layers$rPoints, color = "#ebfc03", opacity = 1, radius = 2, stroke = 1, popup = ~popup,
                       group = "Locations changed during review", options = pathOptions(pane = "points"))
  }
  
  m3
}
```

## Summary of Occurrence Data 
This table includes all records used to generate the map.

```{r occurrenceDataSummary, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
occFiltered <- tables$occFiltered |>
  dplyr::select(dplyr::any_of(c(
    "taxon", "state", "county", "stateFIPS", "countyFIPS", "spatial_match_type",
    "databaseSource", "type", "observerName", "localityInformation", "yearRecorded", "sourceUniqueID"
  ))) |>
  dplyr::rename(
    Taxon = taxon, State = state, County = county,
    `State FIPS` = stateFIPS, `County FIPS` = countyFIPS, `Match Type` = spatial_match_type,
    `Data Source` = databaseSource, Type = type,
    `Collector's Name` = observerName,
    `Locality Description` = localityInformation,
    Year = yearRecorded, `Source ID` = sourceUniqueID
  )

DT::datatable(occFiltered, rownames = FALSE, width = "100%", filter = "top", options = list(scrollX = TRUE))
```

## Data summarized to each County 

The table below displays additional attribute data for all the counties with known occurrences. Columns can be filtered and sorted. The following states are included in this FNA state designation: `r if(meta$fnaIncluded){meta$allStateNames} else {"Not applicable"}`

```{r countyDataTable, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
df <- tables$countySummary |>
  dplyr::select(-dplyr::any_of(c("allColor", "popup", "singleColor"))) |>
  # arrange by state name if available, handle potential column name differences safely
  dplyr::arrange(dplyr::across(dplyr::any_of(c("state", "STATE_NAME", "REGION")))) |>
  dplyr::rename(dplyr::any_of(c(
    `County Name` = "NAME", GEOID = "FIPS", `State Abbreviation` = "state", `State Abbreviation` = "REGION", `State Abbreviation` = "STATE_NAME",
    `iNaturalist Observation` = "O", `Herbarium Occurrence` = "H",
    `Germplasm Occurrence` = "G", `Pre 1970 Records` = "pre1970",
    `All Records Score` = "anyRecord"
  )))

DT::datatable(df, rownames = FALSE, width = "100%", filter = "top", options = list(scrollX = TRUE))
```

## Data excluded by Flora of North America state filter

The above map and table includes all occurrence data that falls within the established state range defined by Flora of North America.

If the state range for a species was defined by Flora of North America all the occurrence records will be shown on the map and above table. 

The table below shows the occurrence data that falls outside the established Flora of North America states.

**The following States are included in the FNA description**: `r if(meta$fnaIncluded){meta$fnaStates} else {"Not included in FNA"}`

```{r fna_exclusion, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
if(!meta$fnaIncluded){
  print("The state range for this species is not present in the Flora of North America")
} else {
  fnaFiltered <- tables$fnaFiltered |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      link = paste0("[https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScCTcg6ITFMXs9QpQEAvN92GI06wtSTiGSTcUBrOK6E2kChjw/viewform?usp=pp_url&entry.1016353547=](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScCTcg6ITFMXs9QpQEAvN92GI06wtSTiGSTcUBrOK6E2kChjw/viewform?usp=pp_url&entry.1016353547=)",
                    taxon, "&entry.764274277=", countyFIPS, "&entry.763749150=", state, 
                    "&entry.1585476570=", county, "&entry.1304312867=", recordID),
      `report issue link` = paste0("<a target='_blank' href='", link, "'>link</a>")
    ) |>
    dplyr::select(dplyr::any_of(c(
      "taxon", "state", "county", "stateFIPS", "countyFIPS", "spatial_match_type",
      "databaseSource", "collectionSource", "type", 
      "report issue link", "localityInformation", "yearRecorded", "latitude", "longitude"
    ))) |>
    # ADDED: Convert all character columns to UTF-8, replacing invalid characters with nothing
    dplyr::mutate(dplyr::across(dplyr::where(is.character), ~ iconv(.x, to = "UTF-8", sub = "")))

  DT::datatable(fnaFiltered, rownames = FALSE, width = "100%", filter = "top", escape = FALSE, options = list(scrollX = TRUE))
}
```
